div.head: 概述
div.p: 109號窟和110號窟爲雙窟，是尺寸標準的方形窟，長、寬和高均略超2米。第一面壁即d壁，有整齊行格。自正壁e壁中段以後，行距和字距開始緊縮，刻字由此增多。
109號窟和110號窟朝南，而卧佛像所在山谷核心區的洞窟，除1號窟和2號窟外均朝北。相較朝北的洞窟，朝南的雙窟刻字漫漶更嚴重，刻字不够清晰，以及部分墻壁上覆蓋着薄薄的苔蘚。這應該是由其靠近1962年竣工的跑馬灘水庫所致。

figure.head: 109號窟全景：窟口及d壁、e壁、f壁（自右至左）。2009年拍攝
Panorama view of Cave 109: cave entrance, walls d, e, and f (from right to left); photograph taken in 2009

div.head: 位置

list.item: H區，109號窟是107號窟至113號窟一排七窟中雙窟的西窟，位於水庫北側。
f壁方位：145°。

div.p: 窟底距崖底3.4米左右，高出水位13米左右。109號窟的西面爲108號窟，兩窟之間的隔墻厚0.68米，東面爲110號窟，其間墻體厚0.55米。
109號窟窟底比108號窟窟底高出0.6米，與110號窟窟底齊平。

div.head: 形制
div.p: 方形敞口平頂窟，長、寬、高均2米餘。f壁尚存雙層窟口的痕迹。窟底大致呈方形，窟頂略微向後下沉。

list.item:
d壁：前端高210厘米，後端高194厘米，
寬196厘米。
e壁：中部高194厘米，寬210厘米。

f壁：前端高215厘米，後端高194厘米，
寬195厘米。
窟口寬：220厘米。

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 門楣前端斷裂。延伸至窟外的窟底前端也有不規則的斷裂痕迹。108號窟、109號窟及110號窟前端均因貫穿三個洞窟的裂縫而處於斷裂的危險之中。108號窟底面破損處呈新月形。109號窟d壁前端63厘米（窟壁2D與3D區之間），臨近窟底處有一道18厘米高、2厘米寬的裂縫，貫穿與110號窟相連的隔墻。

figure.head: 109號窟平面圖。雷魯卡據馬本漢和王平國田野考古繪圖製
Ground plan of Cave 109; drawing by Lukas Ledderose after field map by MB and MS

list.item: 打磨區爲190厘米×190厘米左右，始於窟頂下方9厘米處，止於距e壁9厘米處。打磨區與窟口及窟底之間的界綫已模糊不清。
刻經面爲161厘米×187厘米左右，始於窟頂下方12厘米處，止於距e壁12厘米處。 距窟口約38厘米處，現存刻經的首字位於第18行（1A區）。各行底端的刻字距窟底28厘米。

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 保存現狀不一。d壁前端磨損。近窟口處的墻體底部有一處三角形缺口。窟口布滿青苔，向内延伸80厘米左右。窟底以上100厘米左右，距e壁60厘米左右處，尚存刻字。窟底以上100至125厘米處有一横向區域完全不見刻字。此處上方有一大片紅色區域，距e壁62厘米左右處，其上所刻經文清晰可見。局部區域如5B區及7D區仍可窺見刻經的高超工藝。

row.cell: “［大方便佛報恩經序品第一］”。今已不存。標識《大方便佛報恩經》第一卷序品的起始處。
“［大方］便佛報恩經孝養品第二”。標識《大方便佛報恩經》第一卷第二品孝養品的起始處。
“……今者莫如……”，爲d壁末行最後可見的刻字，出自《大方便佛報恩經》第一卷第二品孝養品。

list.item: 打磨區爲185厘米×195厘米，始於窟頂下方10厘米處，距d壁3厘米；止於距f壁5厘米處；與窟底的距離不可見。
刻經面爲167厘米×183厘米左右，始於窟頂下方12厘米處，距d壁11厘米；止於距離窟底22厘米處，距f壁13厘米。

row.cell:
89行（含正文之前的題記
殘字）
不等，後面部分字數較多

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 保存不良。窟底上方約50至60厘米處有一横向破損面貫穿全壁。此區下方僅有東端及西端幾字可以辨識。此區往上，距窟底約150厘米處，刻經開始可辨。再往上，東端部分刻經可見，西端則難以辨識。距窟底約155至165厘米處，壁面中間一横向65厘米長的一字形區域已經完全剥落。

row.cell: “拒逆可……”。e壁第一處可見的刻字，出自d壁開始刊刻的《大方便佛報恩經》第一卷第二品孝養品。
“［大方便佛報恩經卷第一］”。此行最末，今已不存，標識《大方便佛報恩經》第一卷第二品孝養品的結束。此處據109號窟f壁刻第69行經文標識復原補録。
“［大方便佛報恩經對］治品［第］三……尒時”。標識《大方便佛報恩經》第二卷第三品對治品的開始，亦是第二卷的開始。此處據109號窟f壁刻第69行經文標識補録。
“［大方便佛］報恩［經發］菩提心品第四”。 標識《大方便佛報恩經》第二卷第四品發菩提心品的開始。
“［大方便佛報恩經卷第二］”。今已不存，標識《大方便佛報恩經》第二卷第四品發菩提心品的結束。此處據109號窟f壁刻第69行經文標識復原補録。
“［大方便佛報恩經論議品第五］……”。今已不存，標識《大方便佛報恩經》第三卷第五品論議品的開始。此處據109號窟f壁刻第69行經文標識復原補録。
“承作轉輪王，近來二世”。e壁刻經的最後字句。

list.item: 打磨區約爲174×177厘米，始於窟頂下方9厘米處，距e壁5厘米。打磨區與窟口之間的界限已漫漶莫辨。
刻經面約爲164×172厘米，始於窟頂下方13厘米處，距e壁10厘米處。在第80行距窟口15厘米處，部分刻字仍然清晰可見。刻經最後一排距離窟底32厘米。

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 保存現狀不一。近窟口處墻體有部分缺失。窟壁15C區有一垂直狹溝，寬4至5厘米，從窟底延伸至墻體中部，此爲貫穿108號窟、109號窟及110號窟裂痕的一部分。f壁前端部分漫漶且布滿青苔。窟底上方約96至118厘米處有一水平受潮區域，處於f壁墻面的中間位置，高127厘米，靠近窟口處高達147厘米。受潮面積遍滿整片區域，以致該區域刻經幾乎漶漫不可見。在此區域下方黑化且潮濕的壁面後半部，刻經漶漫。而此區域上方的乳白區域，仍可辨識岩體原本泛紅的色調。距窟底140至150厘米處有一横向裂縫，貫穿墻面。窟底約160至170厘米處的第41行至第66行，有横向的漫漶區域，而在距窟口處約25厘米寬的縱向區域，刻經不可見。窟壁4A區、8B區及12D區保存情况良好，刻經質量之佳依然可見。

row.cell: “［不］作［轉輪王雖不作轉輪王而作］閻浮提……”。標識從e壁續刊的《大方便佛報恩經》第三卷第五品論議品。
“大方便佛報恩經卷第三”。清楚可見，標識《大方便佛報恩經》第三卷第五品論議品的結束。
“大方便佛報恩經惡友品第［六］四”。標識《大方便佛報恩經》第四卷第六品惡友品的開始。此題後有“子”字，或爲帙號。詳見。
“ ……太子所以……”。《大方便佛報恩經》第四卷第六品惡友品現存最後的刻字。此句後各行長度無法辨識。

div.head: 討論
div.bibl:
彭家勝第8頁
作者基於胡文和的研究，測量該窟體積爲11.2立方米。作者辨識刻經爲《大方便佛報恩經》，認爲d壁刊刻第二品“孝養品”，f壁刊刻“惡友品”。作者指出，e壁刻經無法辨識。

胡文和第10頁
胡文和提供的該窟測量數據與彭家勝所提供相同。作者重申f壁刊刻 “惡友品”，但誤認爲是第四品。 因爲經文刻字已風化不清，胡文和未辨識e壁刻經。

胡文和第69頁
胡文和的看法同其此前研究。

李良和鄧之金第45頁
李良和鄧之金提供了此窟及每壁刻經區的測量數據，統計了刻經總字數。作者辨識刻經爲《大方便佛報恩經》，認爲d壁刊刻第二品“孝養品”，而f壁刊刻所謂第四品。

《成都考古發現》第396－397、408頁
成都文物考古研究所在簡報中描述了該窟，提供了所有窟壁測量數據。作者認爲，d壁刊刻“序品”，f壁刊刻第五品“論議品”及第六品“ 惡友品”。作者對比《大正藏》本，把d壁與f壁《大方便佛報恩經》刻經行數排表列出。

李琛妍第41、50、51、53頁，以及第76頁注58
李琛妍將109號窟歸入自己劃分的第七組（即本卷H區）。作者辨識該經爲《大方便佛報恩經》，并稱其爲“以一貫之”（“presented systematically from start to finish”）的經典之一。作者認爲，選刊此經與玄奘在唐顯慶元年（656）將其進獻高宗後日漸流行有關。

曹丹和于春第14－15頁
曹丹和于春提供了此窟壁面的數據，并依據拓片推測刻經總字數：d壁5925字，f壁6840字。作者辨識d壁刻經爲《大方便佛報恩經》“孝養品”，f壁刊刻此經第二卷。

秦臻、張雪芬和雷玉華第38－41頁、第126頁和第454－471頁
作者引用《成都考古發現》。此外，作者提供d壁與f壁刻經位置示意圖，并整理此二壁刻經録文，并按壁面細節加以分析。e壁刻經未提供録文。

肥田路美第111頁
肥田路美大致描述109號窟的位置，并依據d壁刻經拓片，辨識《大方便佛報恩經》第一品及第二品。因風化受損，e壁没有拓片。肥田路美根據彭家勝，辨識f壁爲同部經的第四品。

鄧星亮第81頁和第94頁
鄧星亮提供此窟測量尺寸并大致描述基本情况。作者指出三壁皆有刻經。作者記録d壁刻經爲《大方便佛報恩經》第二品，e壁爲此經第二卷第四品，f壁爲第四卷第六品。作者轉録了刻經可辨識部分的開篇與結尾。

div.head: Overview
div.p: Cave 109, like its twin Cave 110, is a simple dice-shaped box of the standard size, with height, depth, and width, all slightly over 2 m. The very regular grid of columns and rows in the first wall d is cast aside in the middle of wall e, when the columns become tighter and more characters are engraved on the available surface.

figure.head: 109號窟平面圖。雷魯卡據馬本漢和王平國的田野考古繪圖製
Ground plan of Cave 109; drawing by Lukas Ledderose after field map by MB and MS

div.p: Compared to the caves in the core area of the Grove—the valley with the reclining Buddha statue—where all but Caves 1 and 2 face north, the deterioration of the engravings in the south facing Caves 109 and 110 is more advanced. The characters are less crisp and parts of the walls are covered with a thin layer of moss. This may have been caused by the proximity to the Paomatan Reservoir 跑馬灘水庫 built in 1962.

div.head: Location

list.item: Section H, the western of the two sutra caves in a row of seven Caves (107–113) bored into the northern cliff beyond the water reservoir.
Direction of wall f: 145˚.

div.p: The floor lies ca. 3.4 m above the foot of the cliff and ca. 13 m above water level. Cave 109 is separated by a wall, 0.68 m in width, from Cave 108 to the west, and by another wall, 0.55 m in width, from Sutra Cave 110 to the east. The floor of the neighboring Cave 108 is 0.6 m lower than that of Cave 109. The floors of Caves 109 and 110 are on the same level.

div.head: Shape
div.p: Box-shaped with open front; standard size with height, depth, and width, all approximately 2 m; a double entrance frame is still visible in wall f; the ground plan is approximately square, and the ceiling descends slightly towards the rear.

list.item:
Wall d: height at the front 210 cm,
at the rear 194 cm, width 196 cm.

Wall e: height at midpoint 194 cm,
width 210 cm.

Wall f: height at the front 215 cm,
at the rear 194 cm, width 195 cm.
Width of entrance 220 cm.

figure.head: 108號窟和109號窟全景。2015年拍攝
Overview of Caves 108 and 109; photograph taken in 2015

div.head: State of Presentation
div.p: The front part of the lintel is broken off. The floor, which extends outwards from the cave, is also broken off at the front in an irregular line. The front parts of Caves 108, 109, and 110 are endangered by a system of cracks common to all three caves. A crescent-shaped part of the floor of 108 has broken off and fallen down. In Cave 109, an 18 cm high and 2 cm wide crack in wall d near the floor and 63 cm from the front edge (between segments 2D and 3D), leads through the wall into Cave 110.

list.item: A polished area, ca. 190 × 190 cm, begins 9 cm below the ceiling and ends 9 cm from wall e. Its boundaries at the entrance and above the floor are not visible.

The field of script, ca. 161 × 187 cm, begins 12 cm below the ceiling and ends 12 cm before wall e. Ca. 38 cm from the entrance, the first character is preserved in column 18 (segment 1A). The last horizontal row in the field of script ends 28 cm above the floor.

div.head: State of Preservation
div.p: Uneven. The front part of the wall is worn. A triangular piece of stone in the lower part of the wall near the entrance has been lost. A sizable section covered in green moss extends from the full height of the entrance to a point ca. 80 cm into the cave. Characters are still visible in a zone up to a height of ca. 100 cm above the floor, but ending ca. 60 cm from wall e. A horizontal zone ca. 100–125 cm above the floor is completely void of characters. Above this zone, characters are clearly visible in a large reddish area, ending ca. 62 cm from wall e. Segments, such as 5B and 7D, still give an idea of the high quality of the engraving.

row.cell: [大方便佛報恩經序品第一] ; no longer visible; the beginning of chapter 1, entitled Xu pin 序品, coinciding with the beginning of scroll 1 of the Great Skillful Means Sutra on the Buddha’s Repaying Kindness (Da fangbian Fo bao’en jing 大方便佛報恩經; hereafter: Repaying Kindness Sutra).
[大方]便佛報恩經孝養品第二; the beginning of chapter 2, entitled Xiaoyang pin 孝養品, in scroll 1 of the Repaying Kindness Sutra.
……今者莫如……; last visible characters in the last column on wall d, in the chapter 2, entitled Xiaoyang pin 孝養品, in scroll 1 of the Repaying Kindness Sutra.

list.item: A polished area, 185 × 195 cm, begins 10 cm below the ceiling and 3 cm from wall d. It ends 5 cm from wall f. The distance to the floor is not visible.
The field of script, ca. 167 × 183 cm, begins 12 cm below the ceiling and 11 cm from wall d. It ends 22 cm above the floor and 13 cm before wall f.

div.head: State of Preservation
div.p: Poor. A damaged zone extends horizontally over the entire wall at a height of ca. 50–60 cm above the floor. Beneath that zone, only a few characters are visible at the western and eastern edges of the wall. Above this damaged zone, characters start to become recognizable up to ca. 150 cm above the floor. Still higher, characters are visible in the east, but hardly at all in the west. In the center of the wall, ca. 155–165 cm above the floor, a horizontal, 65 cm long piece of the wall’s surface, in the shape of a large scale character yi 一, has completely flaked away.

row.cell: uneven; higher numbers in the rear part
ca. 0.2–0.5 cm (front part); ca. 0.1–0.3 cm (rear part)
……能[龍？]開十……; fragments of a colophon.
拒逆可……; the first visible characters of the sutra text on wall e, in the chapter 2, entitled Xiaoyang pin 孝養品, of scroll 1 in the Repaying Kindness Sutra, continuing from wall d.

[大方便佛報恩經卷第一]; at the end of the column; no longer visible; refers to the end of scroll 1, coinciding with the end of chapter 2, entitled Xiaoyang pin 孝養品, in the Repaying Kindness Sutra. Recon­struc­tion by analogy with Cave 109, wall f, column 69.

[大方便佛報恩經對]治品[第]三……尒時; the beginning of chapter 3, entitled Duizhi pin 對治品, coinciding with the beginning of scroll 2 of the Repaying Kindness Sutra. Recon­struc­tion by analogy with Cave 109, wall f, column 69.
[大方便佛]報恩[經發]菩提心品第四; the beginning of chapter 4, entitled Fa putixin pin 發菩提心品, in scroll 2 of the Repaying Kindness Sutra.

[大方便佛報恩經卷第二]; no longer visible; the end of scroll 2, coinciding with the end of chapter 4, entitled Fa putixin pin 發菩提心品, in the Repaying Kindness Sutra. Recon­struc­tion by analogy with Cave 109, wall f, column 69.

[大方便佛報恩經論議品第五] ; no longer visible; the beginning of chapter 5, entitled Lunyi pin 論議品, coinciding with the beginning of scroll 3 in the Repaying Kindness Sutra. Re­con­struc­tion by analogy with Cave 109, wall f, column 69.
承作轉輪王，近來二世; the last characters on wall e.

list.item: A polished area, ca. 174 × 177 cm, begins 9 cm below the ceiling and 5 cm from wall e. Its boundaries at the entrance and above the floor are no longer visible.

The field of script, ca. 164 × 172 cm, begins 13 cm below the ceiling and 10 cm from wall e. Some characters in column 80 are still visible 15 cm from the entrance opening. The last visible horizontal row in the field of script is 32 cm above the floor.

div.head: State of Preservation
div.p: Uneven. Near the entrance, parts of the wall have been lost. In segment 15C a vertical slot, 4–5 cm wide, runs up from the floor. This is part of the system of cracks in the frontal part of Caves 108, 109, and 110. The front part of wall f is worn and covered with green moss. A horizontal moist zone, in which nearly all characters are effaced, runs across the entire width of the wall, about 96–118 cm above the floor, bulging in the middle to a height of 127 cm, and near the entrance reaching up to 147 cm. In the blackish, moist areas below that zone, most characters are effaced in the rear part of the wall. In the cream-colored field of script above that zone, the original reddish tinge of the stone can still be recognized. A horizontal crack runs through the wall at a height of 140–150 cm. A horizontal effaced patch extends from columns 41–66 at a height of ca. 160–170 cm above the floor. Characters in a ca. 25 cm wide vertical zone at the entrance have disappeared. The quality can still be seen in some better preserved segments, such as 4A, 8B, and 12D.

row.cell: [不]作[轉輪王雖不作轉輪王而作]閻浮提……; characters in chapter 5, entitled Lunyi pin 論議品, in scroll 3 of the Repaying Kindness Sutra continuing from wall e.
大方便佛報恩經卷第三 ; visible, end of scroll 3 in the Repaying Kindness Sutra, coinciding with the end of chapter 5, entitled Lunyi pin 論議品.

大方便佛報恩經惡友品第[六] 四; beginning of chapter 6, entitled Eyou pin 惡友品, coinciding with the beginning of scroll 4 of the Repaying Kindness Sutra. There­after follows the single character zi 子, which could be the number of the wrapper.See .
……太子所以……; last visible characters in the chapter 6, entitled Eyou pin 惡友品, of scroll 4 in the Repaying Kindness Sutra. Thereafter the lengths of columns are no longer recognizable.

note.p: Peng (based on Hu Wenhe’s work) gives measurements of the cave and calculates its volume at 11.2 cubic meters. He identifies the Repaying Kindness Sutra and says that wall d includes the second Chapter on Filial Devotion (Xiaoyang pin 孝養品), and wall f the Chapter on Bad Friend (Eyou pin 惡友品). He writes that it is unclear which text was carved on wall e.

Hu Wenhe gives the measurements of the cave, following . He repeats Peng’s identification of the Chapter on Bad Friend (Eyou pin 惡友品) on wall f, but erroneously numbers it as chapter 4. He does not identify the text carved on wall e because of its erosion.
Hu Wenhe repeats the information in his earlier publication, .

The authors give measurements for the cave and for the fields of script on each wall, also calculating the total number of carved characters. They note that the Repaying Kindness Sutra’s second Chapter on Filial Devotion (Xiaoyang pin 孝養品) is carved on wall d, and the alleged fourth chapter on wall f.

The editors of the Chengdu Municipal Institute of Cultural Relics and Archaeology give measurements and a detailed description of the cave. They write that the first Preliminary Chapter (Xu pin 序品) was carved on wall d, and on wall f the fifth Chapter on Doctrinal Discussion (Lunyi pin 論議品) and the sixth Chapter on Bad Friend (Eyou pin 惡友品). They tabulate the column numbers for these chapters of the Repaying Kindness Sutra carved on walls d and f, with reference to the Taishō edition.

Lee includes Cave 109 in her cluster VII, which corresponds to section H. She identifies the Repaying Kindness Sutra and writes that it is one of the scriptures “presented systematically from start to finish.” She explains the appearance of this text at Wofo Yuan with its growing popularity after Xuanzang had presented a copy of it to Emperor Gaozong in 656.

Cao and Yu give measurements of the walls, their surface areas, and, based on rubbings, the number of extant characters: wall d, 5,925; wall f, 6,840. They identify the second Chapter on Filial Devotion (Xiaoyang pin 孝養品) of the Repaying Kindness Sutra on wall d, and the second scroll of the same sutra on wall f.

The authors repeat the information in . In addition, they provide diagrams of walls d and f and transcribe the engraved text on these walls, which they analyze according to their own detailed scheme. They do not transcribe the text on wall e.

Hida describes the location of the Cave 109. On the basis of the rubbing of the text carved on wall d, she identifies the first and second chapter of the Repaying Kindness Sutra. Due to erosion, no rubbing is available for wall e. Following , Hida identifies the fourth chapter of the same sutra on wall f.

Deng Xingliang gives measurements and a general description of the cave. He notes that sutra texts were carved on all three walls. He records that wall d is engraved, with chapter 2 of the Repaying Kindness Sutra; wall e is engraved with chapter 4 from the second scroll of the same sutra, and wall f with chapter 6 from the fourth scroll. He transcribes the beginning and the end of the legible passages.
